codeReview.summary: All three prior blocking issues remain UNFIXED: (1) Backend API endpoints for upload, delete, self-assessment, and reminders are missing from router; (2) MedicalSelfAssessment and Reminder models are missing from models.py; (3) Stage enum mismatch between frontend ('new_application'|'admit_card_released'|'dv_scheduled') and backend ('pst'|'pet'|'medical'|'document_verification'). Additionally, a new issue was found: duplicate router registration in main.py.

issues.description: Missing backend API endpoints: The frontend calls POST /documents/upload, DELETE /documents/{documentId}, GET /documents/medical/self-assessment, POST /documents/medical/self-assessment, POST /documents/reminders, GET /documents/reminders/me, DELETE /documents/reminders/{reminderId} - none of which exist in the backend router. Only these endpoints exist: GET /documents/checklists, GET /documents/me, GET /documents/readiness, PUT /documents/checklists/{item_id}/status, GET /documents/medical/guidelines.

Missing database models: No models exist for MedicalSelfAssessment or Reminder entities. The frontend defines MedicalSelfAssessment and Reminder interfaces in documents.ts, and the service layer references them but no corresponding database models exist.

Stage enum mismatch: Frontend uses DocumentStage = 'new_application' | 'admit_card_released' | 'dv_scheduled' but backend (apps/api/internal/documents/models.py) uses DocumentStage = 'pst' | 'pet' | 'medical' | 'document_verification'. These stage names are completely different and will cause all stage-based filtering and display to fail.
Duplicate router registration: documents_router is included twice - once at line 65 and again at lines 116-117. This is a minor issue but indicates incomplete code cleanup.

Medical guidelines are hardcoded static data in get_medical_guidelines() instead of being queried from a database. The method returns hardcoded UUIDs and static content rather than querying a medical_guidelines table.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend worker added i18n translations for documents module but did not document the required stage enum mapping. The mismatch between frontend stages ('new_application', 'admit_card_released', 'dv_scheduled') and backend stages ('pst', 'pet', 'medical', 'document_verification') should have been caught and resolved.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/src/lib/api/documents.ts:6 defines DocumentStage as 'new_application'|'admit_card_released'|'dv_scheduled' but apps/api/internal/documents/models.py:17 defines DocumentStage as 'pst'|'pet'|'medical'|'document_verification'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill does not include guidance on coordinating stage enum values with backend. The worker implemented the frontend correctly according to the feature description (which mentions 'New Application, Admit Card Released, DV Scheduled') but did not verify backend alignment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker created frontend stages based on feature description without checking backend enum values

summary: The document-readiness-module implementation FAILS review. All three prior blocking issues remain unfixed: (1) Backend endpoints for upload, delete, self-assessment, and reminders are completely missing; (2) MedicalSelfAssessment and Reminder database models do not exist; (3) Stage enums are mismatched between frontend and backend. The frontend component is implemented but cannot function without the corresponding backend API. Additionally, the stage enum mismatch will cause all stage-based document filtering to fail at runtime.
